_comment: Layer definitions + per-layer company appearances (the report's data tables). Curated/sourced as of the May 2026 coverage date. segment_rev_note = the AI-relevant segment revenue and its share of total company revenue (or 'pure play'). key_contracts = major deals WITH durations. backlog_rpo = contracted backlog. Longer prose lives in data/narrative.py. Re-run 2_seed_companies.py after editing.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Copilot revenue is bundled inside MSFT's Productivity + Cloud segments (not broken out). M365 Copilot ~$5B+ run-rate ($30/seat); GitHub Copilot ~$450–850M ARR (4.7M paid subs, 90% of F100).
company_layer.key_contracts: Bundled into M365 E5; GitHub Copilot Business/Enterprise seats.
company_layer.note: Only large-cap with direct, monetized AI-app exposure at scale.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Gemini App + Workspace AI bundled within Google Services / Cloud; not separately disclosed. Embedded across Search, Workspace, Android.
company_layer.key_contracts: Workspace AI add-ons; Gemini consumer subscriptions.
company_layer.note: Distribution moat (billions of users) but app revenue not yet broken out.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Owns Gemini outright (100%) and the full stack — model + TPU + Google Cloud. The only frontier lab that owns its own accelerator and cloud, which is why Cloud margins inflected.
company_layer.key_contracts: Gemini 3 across Vertex AI; TPU-trained (vertically integrated).
company_layer.note: Only frontier-lab proxy that owns its full stack (model + TPU + cloud).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Holds a ~27% economic stake in OpenAI (~$135B, post-PBC restructure Oct'25) + IP rights to 2032; also builds in-house Phi / MAI models. Cleanest public OpenAI proxy.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI: 27% economic stake + $250B Azure; IP rights to 2032.
company_layer.note: Best public proxy for OpenAI economics.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Largest outside shareholder of Anthropic — ~$46B+ invested ($8B+$13B+$25B); convertible/non-voting, est. high-teens % economic, non-controlling. Also owns Nova models. Cleanest Anthropic proxy.
company_layer.key_contracts: Anthropic: $8B+$13B+$25B equity + $100B AWS compute.
company_layer.note: Best public proxy for Anthropic economics.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Owns Llama outright (open-weight). Monetized indirectly via ad-targeting uplift, not API fees — so there is no clean 'LLM revenue' line.
company_layer.key_contracts: Llama 4 open weights; in-house MTIA accelerators.
company_layer.note: Monetizes models through its ad engine, not API fees.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Owns Hunyuan (100%); #1 Chinese lab by revenue. Embedded across WeChat / Tencent Cloud.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hunyuan across WeChat ecosystem.
company_layer.note: Distribution via WeChat's ~1.4B users.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Owns Ernie (100%); monetized via search + Baidu AI Cloud.
company_layer.key_contracts: Ernie across Baidu search + cloud.
company_layer.note: China search incumbent's LLM play.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Microsoft Cloud $54.5B in FQ3'26 (+29% YoY); Intelligent Cloud segment $34.7B (+30%). Azure $ not separately disclosed; Azure growth +40% (+39% cc). AI run-rate >$37B (+123%). Microsoft Cloud ≈ ~half of MSFT's $281.7B FY25 (Jun) revenue.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI: 27% stake (~$135B) + $250B Azure commitment (no longer right-of-first-refusal). Anthropic: $30B Azure + MSFT $5B (up to $10B) equity.
company_layer.note: Azure $ is estimated industry-wide (~$30B+/qtr) since MSFT discloses only growth %.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: AWS FY25 $128.7B (+19%), op income $45.6B (35.4% margin). Q4'25 $35.6B → Q1'26 $37.6B (+5.6% QoQ, +28% YoY); $150B annualized run-rate. AWS ≈ 18% of Amazon's $716.9B FY25 revenue but ~57% of consolidated operating income.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI $38B / 7-yr (first AWS–OpenAI deal, Nov'25). Anthropic ~$100B AWS (multi-yr) + Project Rainier (1M+ Trainium2, Indiana).
company_layer.note: AWS posted its fastest growth in 15 quarters in Q1'26.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Google Cloud FY25 ~$59B (Q4'25 $17.7B, +48%); Q1'26 $20.0B (+63% YoY, +13% QoQ); Cloud op income $6.6B (33% margin) vs $2.2B a year ago. Cloud ≈ 15% of Alphabet's $402.8B FY25 revenue.
company_layer.key_contracts: Anthropic: $3B equity (~14% stake) + tens of $B for 1M+ TPUs (Ironwood 7th-gen, 1+ GW).
company_layer.note: Cloud margin inflected sharply (op income 3x YoY).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Total cloud $8.9B in Q3'FY26 (+44%): OCI infrastructure $4.9B (+84% YoY), cloud apps $4.0B (+13%). Cloud is the majority of Oracle's $57.4B FY25 (May) revenue.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI Stargate ~$300B / 5-yr from 2027 (Abilene TX + 5 new sites).
company_layer.backlog_rpo: RPO $553B (+325% YoY; +$29B QoQ) — mostly large-scale AI contracts
company_layer.note: FY26 guide $67B total; FY27 raised to $90B; FY30 OCI-alone target $144B. RPO ≈ 8x current revenue.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Alibaba Cloud ~$5B/quarter, ~10% of Alibaba's $150.9B FY (Mar'26) revenue; ~4% global cloud share (largest non-Western). AI-related product revenue +triple-digit YoY for 11 straight quarters.
company_layer.key_contracts: Qwen open-weight models; cloud bundled with AI.
company_layer.note: Group margins (op ~6%) reflect e-commerce mix, not cloud-only economics.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Pure-play GPUaaS. FY25 revenue $5.13B (fastest cloud platform ever to $5B). TTM GM ~69%, op margin ~ -7% (heavy D&A/SBC), EBITDA margin ~+49%.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI $22.4B through May 2031 (Mar'25 $11.9B/5-yr + May'25 $4B + Sep'25 $6.5B). Microsoft $10B+. Meta ~$14B. NVDA $2B equity.
company_layer.note: Highly customer-concentrated (Microsoft + OpenAI dominate).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: European challenger. FY25 revenue ~$0.5B but ARR exited 2025 at $900M–$1.1B (Q2 rev +625% YoY). TTM GM ~72%.
company_layer.key_contracts: Microsoft $17.4B through 2031 (expandable to ~$19.4B). Meta ~$3B / 5-yr. NVDA $2B equity.
company_layer.note: Revenue tiny today; story is the contracted ramp.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Ex-bitcoin miner pivoting to AI cloud; FY ends Jun. Owns power + builds liquid-cooled DC at Childress TX (750MW campus).
company_layer.key_contracts: Microsoft $9.7B / 5-yr (20% prepayment; NVIDIA GB300; ~$1.94B annualized run-rate once fully commissioned, phased through 2026).
company_layer.note: Self-operates AI cloud in addition to the Microsoft hosting deal.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: HPC revenue ($21M in Q1'26) overtook bitcoin ($13M) for the first time — the pivot inflection point.
company_layer.key_contracts: Fluidstack (Google-backstopped): 200+MW 10-yr (~$3.7B) + two 5-yr extensions → up to $8.7B; plus a 25-yr lease (~$9.5B, Oct'25). ~$12.8B contracted total.
company_layer.note: Google provides a backstop/guarantee on the Fluidstack lease obligations.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Bitcoin miner pivoting to AI DC leasing.
company_layer.key_contracts: Fluidstack (Google-backed): River Bend 245MW 15-yr ($7.0B) + Beacon Point 352MW 15-yr ($9.8B), both with 3% annual rent escalators.
company_layer.note: Beacon Point is phase 1 of a planned 1 GW campus.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Bitcoin miner pivoting to AI DC leasing.
company_layer.key_contracts: AWS: 300MW 15-yr $5.5B lease (Barber Lake / Fortress campus, TX) for turnkey space + power.
company_layer.note: First major AWS hosting deal with an ex-miner.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~Pure DC REIT. FY25 revenue $9.2B; record interconnection. AFFO growth 9–13%.
company_layer.key_contracts: 260k+ interconnections; >10k customers across 270+ DCs.
company_layer.note: Interconnection (network density) is the durable moat, not just space.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~Pure DC REIT. FY25 revenue $6.1B; 769MW under construction (Q4'25).
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler powered-shell leases; record bookings.
company_layer.backlog_rpo: Multi-year leased backlog; >$1B annual bookings run-rate
company_layer.note: #1 wholesale; single-tenant hyperscale capacity.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: DC + grid electrical work is a fast-growing slice of Quanta's ~$25B revenue (Electric Power segment); largest US DC electrical contractor.
company_layer.key_contracts: Multi-year MSAs with utilities + hyperscalers.
company_layer.note: Picks-and-shovels on the buildout; not AI-pure but heavily AI-driven now.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data centers / tech are now a large and rising share of Comfort Systems' backlog (~$8B revenue); modular + DC mechanical.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler + chip-fab construction.
company_layer.backlog_rpo: Record backlog; DC + advanced-tech mix rising
company_layer.note: High-teens ROE; benefits from DC + semiconductor fab buildout.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: AVGO AI semiconductor FY25 $20B (+65%); networking (Tomahawk/Jericho switch ASICs) is a major component. AI switch backlog >$10B; Tomahawk 6 = 102 Tbps. AI semis ≈ 31% of AVGO's $64B FY25 total.
company_layer.key_contracts: Powers most third-party Ethernet switches; AI-networking design wins across hyperscalers.
company_layer.note: Also the custom-ASIC leader (see L4). Networking + custom XPU together drive the AI story.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~Pure DC networking (>92% DC-focused). FY25 revenue $9.0B; ~19% DC switch share. Meta + Microsoft are anchor customers.
company_layer.key_contracts: Meta + Microsoft multi-year design wins; 800G ramping to 1.6T.
company_layer.note: Cleanest large-cap merchant-switching pure-play; 43% op margin.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: DC switching is a modest slice of Cisco's ~$56B revenue; 14% DC switch share, 29.8% total Ethernet. AI orders from webscale crossed $1B+ run-rate.
company_layer.key_contracts: Webscale AI infrastructure orders (>$1B); Acacia coherent optics.
company_layer.note: Diversified incumbent — AI is incremental, not the whole story.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: DC networking (Spectrum-X Ethernet + InfiniBand + NVLink) sits inside NVIDIA's $193.7B DC revenue; networking +142% YoY in FY26. ~15% DC Ethernet share.
company_layer.key_contracts: Bundled with GPU clusters; Spectrum-X adopted by neoclouds + hyperscalers.
company_layer.note: Networking is NVIDIA's fastest-growing DC line; reinforces the rack-scale moat.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data center ~75% of Marvell's $8.2B FY26 (Jan) revenue; optical DSPs lead the market. Also custom-ASIC (see L4).
company_layer.key_contracts: Optical DSP design wins; AWS Trainium / Microsoft Maia silicon.
company_layer.note: AI ≈ 50% of data-center revenue and rising.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Datacom/AI optics now the growth core. FY25 (Jun) $1.6B understates run-rate — TTM revenue +90%, GM ~41%, op margin ~22%. 50–60% global EML share; OCS leader.
company_layer.key_contracts: NVDA $2B equity (Mar'26) — funds US fab + multibillion NVIDIA purchase commitment + future capacity-access rights (non-exclusive).
company_layer.note: FY label is stale vs the optics inflection — use TTM. Stock +1,474% LTM; valuation extreme (fwd P/E ~50x).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Datacom transceivers a fast-growing share of Coherent's $5.8B FY25 (Jun); InP laser capacity sold out through 2027. TTM GM ~37%.
company_layer.key_contracts: NVDA $2B equity (Mar'26) — same structure as Lumentum (US-fab funding + purchase commitment + capacity rights).
company_layer.note: Diversified (industrial lasers + networking); datacom is the AI lever.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 800G/1.6T datacom transceivers for hyperscalers; explosive ramp (stock +441% YTD). Smallest, highest-beta optics name.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler 800G design wins.
company_layer.note: High operational risk; execution + customer concentration.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Optical Communications ~$5B+ of Corning's ~$14–15B total revenue; Q1'26 optical comm $1.8B (+36%). Enterprise/AI fiber driving the mix up.
company_layer.key_contracts: Meta $6B+ multi-year fiber agreement (Jan'26).
company_layer.note: Diversified (Display, Specialty, Auto) — optical is the AI-levered segment.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Pure-play PCIe/CXL/Ethernet retimers + fabric ICs for AI racks. ~Pure AI play.
company_layer.key_contracts: Designed into hyperscaler + GPU rack-scale platforms.
company_layer.note: High-growth, high-multiple; benefits from rack-scale scale-up.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Active Electrical Cables + SerDes for hyperscaler racks; ~pure AI play, hyperscaler-direct.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler AEC design wins (Microsoft, Amazon).
company_layer.note: Copper-interconnect complement to optics inside the rack.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: AI & Cloud (data-center networking + cloud) a portion of Nokia's ~€19B revenue; Q1'26 AI & Cloud +49% YoY.
company_layer.key_contracts: NVDA $1B equity (2.9% stake, Oct'25); NVIDIA AI-RAN partnership; T-Mobile USA AI-RAN pilot.
company_layer.note: #2 RAN ex-China; the only major telecom name with an NVIDIA AI-RAN tie.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: #1 RAN ex-China but no NVIDIA partnership; founding AI-RAN Alliance member. Flat 2026 RAN outlook.
company_layer.key_contracts: AI-RAN Alliance (no NVIDIA equity tie).
company_layer.note: Included as the AI-RAN contrast to Nokia; lost the NVIDIA tie-up.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: AI servers a fast-growing slice of Foxconn's ~$220B revenue; cloud & networking ~40% of group. Lead NVIDIA GB200/GB300 rack ODM (part of 59.4% combined ODM share). Razor-thin ~6% gross margin.
company_layer.key_contracts: Primary NVIDIA rack ODM; hyperscaler direct-buy assembly.
company_layer.note: Volume game — margins compress as scale rises; pricing power ~2/10.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: AI server $25B+ FY26 run-rate within Dell's $113.5B FY26 (Jan) revenue (ISG segment); group GM ~22%, but AI-server margin is lower than traditional server.
company_layer.key_contracts: Enterprise + neocloud + sovereign AI server wins.
company_layer.note: Branded OEM squeezed between ODMs below and GPU costs above.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~Pure-play AI server maker; ~9.5% server share. FY (Jun). Q4 revenue +134% YoY but GM only ~9.7% and falling.
company_layer.key_contracts: Liquid-cooled rack-scale NVIDIA systems.
company_layer.note: Highest revenue beta to GPU cycles but thinnest margins + governance overhang.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: DC power (PSUs) + cooling + power management a growing share of Delta's ~$13–14B revenue; #1 AI-server PSU supplier.
company_layer.key_contracts: PSUs for NVIDIA GB200/GB300 racks.
company_layer.note: Higher margin than ODMs; 100+kW racks require its high-efficiency PSUs.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data Center $193.7B = 89.7% of NVIDIA's $215.9B FY26 (Jan) revenue (91.5% in Q4). 86–90% AI accelerator share. 73% gross margin.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI up to $100B / 10 GW (still LOI). Plus equity in CRWV/NBIS/CBRS/LITE/COHR/NOK/xAI (see Deal Web).
company_layer.backlog_rpo: Blackwell sold out 12+ months; Rubin booked
company_layer.note: Single largest profit pool in the chain — ~$120B net income captures ~30% of all AI capex as profit.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data Center segment $16.6B FY25 (+32%) = 48% of AMD's $34.6B total revenue (Instinct GPU + EPYC CPU; Instinct not broken out). ~5–7% AI accelerator share.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI 6 GW MI450 (first 1 GW H2'26) + 160M-share warrant (~10% of AMD at $0.01/share, vesting to $600).
company_layer.note: Margins below NVIDIA; supply gated by TSMC CoWoS allocation.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: AI semiconductor $20B FY25 (+65%; >half of semi revenue; 31% of $64B total). Custom XPUs for Google TPU, Meta MTIA + others. ~50% AI op margin.
company_layer.key_contracts: OpenAI 10 GW custom silicon (through 2030); Anthropic multi-GW (Apr'26). Two new XPU customers ramping.
company_layer.note: The ASIC substitution thesis — hyperscalers' answer to NVIDIA pricing.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data center ~75% of Marvell's $8.2B FY26 revenue; AI ~50% of DC. AWS Trainium + Microsoft Maia custom silicon.
company_layer.key_contracts: AWS Trainium, Microsoft Maia custom programs.
company_layer.note: Smaller custom player than Broadcom; also optics (L6).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Pure-play wafer-scale inference. FY25 revenue ~$510M (+76%); IPO'd May'26 (+68% day 1). Extreme valuation (P/E ~580x, P/S ~100x); not yet GAAP-profitable group-wide.
company_layer.key_contracts: $10B OpenAI / 750MW (via AWS Bedrock partnership).
company_layer.note: Niche inference architecture; highly speculative, customer-concentrated.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Enterprise/compute (incl. GPU power delivery) a major and growing share of MPWR revenue; critical voltage regulation for every GPU rack.
company_layer.key_contracts: Designed into NVIDIA + AMD GPU power trains.
company_layer.note: Adjacent but essential — power delivery scales with rack density.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Gaudi 3 ~1–2% AI accelerator share; immaterial to Intel's revenue. Story is foundry (L2), not merchant AI chips.
company_layer.note: Included for completeness; not a credible NVIDIA/AMD challenger in merchant GPU.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: FY25 revenue ₩97.1T (~$64.5B, +~50% YoY); HBM revenue more than doubled in 2025. 57% HBM share; ~70% of HBM4 for NVIDIA Rubin; 34% DRAM (took #1). Net profit doubled.
company_layer.key_contracts: Sole HBM3E for NVIDIA H100/H200; lead HBM4 supplier for Rubin.
company_layer.note: Purest HBM pure-play; peak-cycle earnings → fwd P/E ~5x. Reports in KRW.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Memory is a portion of Samsung's ~₩300T+ revenue (also smartphones, displays, foundry). 22% HBM, 33% DRAM. HBM4 sampling/qualifying.
company_layer.key_contracts: HBM3E recovery; HBM4 qualification underway.
company_layer.note: Diversified conglomerate — memory upside diluted by handset/display. Also #2 foundry (L2).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: FY25 (Aug) $37.4B; HBM ramp pushing group GM up sharply. 21% HBM, 26% DRAM; data center >50% of revenue. Sold out 2026.
company_layer.key_contracts: 12-Hi HBM3E for NVIDIA; HBM4 qualifying (BESI sole-source bonder).
company_layer.note: Only US-listed pure memory maker; fwd P/E ~8x on surging earnings (+756% TTM).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Spun from WDC Feb'25; FY25 (Jun) $7.4B distorted by spin (net loss, neg. EBITDA margin). TTM tells the real story: revenue +251%, GM ~56% as NAND contract prices +246% in 2025. HBF (high-bandwidth flash) AI thesis emerging.
company_layer.key_contracts: Storage sold out through 2026.
company_layer.note: Use TTM not FY25 — the FY straddles the spin-off. Stock +492% YTD; cycle risk high.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Post-Sandisk-spin, ~pure HDD + platforms. Nearline HDD is the cheap mass-storage tier for AI 'data lakes'. Sold out 2026.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler nearline HDD LTAs.
company_layer.note: +850% LTM; 3-player HDD oligopoly priced briefly like a monopoly.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: HDD oligopoly (only 3 players); Mozaic HAMR high-capacity drives for AI mass storage.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler nearline HDD LTAs.
company_layer.note: +600% LTM; same cycle thesis as WDC.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: HPC (incl. AI) = 61% of TSMC's Q1'26 revenue (up from 51% a year ago); HPC +20% QoQ. FY25 $121.2B; 69.9% pure-play foundry share; sole CoWoS advanced packaging.
company_layer.key_contracts: Manufactures NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Apple, hyperscaler ASICs.
company_layer.note: Highest-quality monopoly in the chain; 60% GM, ~$54B FY25 net income. ADR price USD, financials TWD.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Foundry is a small part of Samsung; 7.2% share; 3nm yield improving, 2nm + HBM4 in focus.
company_layer.key_contracts: Tesla/Qualcomm + internal; chasing 2nm wins.
company_layer.note: Only credible #2 leading-edge foundry but yield-disadvantaged vs TSMC.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 5.3% pure-play foundry share; restricted to 7nm/5nm-class by US export controls (no EUV). China's leading-edge champion.
company_layer.key_contracts: Huawei + domestic China chips.
company_layer.note: Geopolitically central; capped by equipment access. Reports USD/CNY.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 18A ramping; sub-scale at leading edge vs TSMC. US strategic asset (10% US government stake).
company_layer.key_contracts: Internal + early external 18A customers; US CHIPS support.
company_layer.note: Turnaround optionality, not yet an AI-foundry force.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Advanced packaging a growing share of ASE's ~$18B revenue; #1 OSAT; gets CoWoS overflow from TSMC.
company_layer.key_contracts: CoWoS-overflow + non-AI packaging.
company_layer.note: Leveraged to advanced-packaging capacity expansion.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: #2 OSAT (~$7B revenue); Arizona facility co-located near TSMC's US fabs for advanced packaging.
company_layer.key_contracts: US advanced-packaging (TSMC-adjacent).
company_layer.note: US onshore packaging beneficiary.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: EDA + IP + (now Ansys) simulation; ~35% EDA share; every AI chip is designed in it. ~95% incremental gross margins (software). Broadly AI-exposed; ~35% op margin.
company_layer.key_contracts: +Ansys $35B (closed Jul'25). NVIDIA equity investment ($2B, per NVDA portfolio).
company_layer.note: Toll-booth on every chip design; duopoly with Cadence.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~30% EDA share; gen-AI placement/routing tools. Same software economics as Synopsys.
company_layer.key_contracts: Design-tool standard across fabless + hyperscaler ASIC teams.
company_layer.note: Duopoly partner to Synopsys; high recurring revenue.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Royalties + licensing on virtually every AI device; Arm cores in NVIDIA Grace, AWS Graviton, Google Axion, mobile. FY (Mar).
company_layer.key_contracts: Architecture licenses to NVIDIA + hyperscalers; v9 + CSS for data center.
company_layer.note: Royalty model compounds as Arm-based DC CPUs proliferate.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Sole EUV maker; FY25 ~$38B revenue; 51% GM. China ~20–30% of revenue (export-control risk). High-NA EUV $380M/unit, 18-month lead times.
company_layer.key_contracts: Every leading-edge fab (TSMC, Samsung, Intel) buys EUV.
company_layer.backlog_rpo: Multi-year EUV order backlog; targets ~$71B rev by 2030
company_layer.note: Most defensible monopoly in the chain (pricing power 10/10).

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Broadest WFE portfolio (~18% WFE share). AI-leverage via logic + DRAM/HBM capex; not separately broken out.
company_layer.key_contracts: All major fabs + memory makers.
company_layer.note: Most diversified equipment exposure to the buildout.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Etch leader; #1 memory equipment — directly levered to HBM/DRAM/NAND capex surge. ~11% WFE share.
company_layer.key_contracts: Memory makers (SK Hynix, Micron, Samsung) + logic.
company_layer.note: Highest memory-capex beta among WFE names.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~50% metrology/inspection share (~7% of total WFE); yield-critical as nodes shrink. ~50% segment GM.
company_layer.key_contracts: Every leading-edge + advanced-packaging line.
company_layer.note: Yield economics make metrology non-discretionary.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~13% WFE share; coat/develop near-monopoly + etch; EUV-adjacent process tools. Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: All EUV-capable fabs.
company_layer.note: EUV ramp pulls coater/developer demand.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Dominant HBM + AI-SoC test (ATE); directly levered to HBM stack counts + complex AI chips. Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: Memory + logic test for HBM/AI.
company_layer.note: More HBM stacks + bigger chips = more test intensity.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~Monopoly in EUV photomask actinic defect inspection; pure-play to EUV adoption. Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: EUV mask shops + leading-edge fabs.
company_layer.note: Tiny but unique EUV-ecosystem monopoly.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Back-end dicing/grinding niche monopolist; essential to advanced packaging (CoWoS) volume. Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: OSATs + foundry advanced packaging.
company_layer.note: Levered to advanced-packaging unit growth.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~71% global HBM TC-bonder share; pure-play to HBM stacking. Reports KRW. Tiny base (~$0.8B FY+1 est), huge growth.
company_layer.key_contracts: SK Hynix + memory HBM lines.
company_layer.note: Purest HBM-capacity pure-play; high cyclicality.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Hybrid bonding leader (next-gen HBM4/advanced packaging); Micron HBM4 sole-source TC bonder. Reports EUR.
company_layer.key_contracts: Micron HBM4 (sole-source bonder).
company_layer.note: Hybrid bonding is the next packaging inflection.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Photomask blanks are a portion of Hoya's revenue (also eyeglass lenses + medical/endoscopes); 60%+ photomask, 75%+ EUV blanks, only validated High-NA blank supplier. Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: Every EUV mask shop depends on Hoya blanks.
company_layer.note: Single-country (Japan) tail risk on a 75%-share input.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: #1 silicon wafer (~33% share) + leading photoresist; broad chemicals conglomerate (PVC too). Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: All fabs (wafers + EUV photoresist).
company_layer.note: Diversified; semiconductor materials are the AI-levered piece.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Nov'25 DuPont spinoff; ~$4.7B revenue in CMP slurries, photoresists, advanced-packaging materials.
company_layer.key_contracts: Fabs + OSATs (consumable materials).
company_layer.note: New pure-play on semiconductor consumable materials.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: ~Pure DC infrastructure. FY25 $10.2B (+~30%); Q4'25 orders +252% YoY. Liebert power + thermal; 360AI reference designs with NVIDIA.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler + neocloud power/thermal; NVIDIA 360AI co-design.
company_layer.note: Cleanest large-cap DC-physical-infra pure-play; tied #1 in cooling.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data centers ~16% of Schneider's $46.7B revenue (~$7.6B); #1 in switchgear and UPS, also cooling (Motivair immersion). Reports EUR.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler electrical distribution + UPS.
company_layer.note: Diversified electrification giant — DC is the fastest-growing slice.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Data center ~$8B and rising of Eaton's electrical revenue; switchgear + transformers with multi-year backlogs.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler + utility electrical equipment.
company_layer.note: Transformer/switchgear lead times create pricing power.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Gas turbines + HVDC grid equipment; DC orders $2.4B in Q1'26. Spun from GE Apr'24.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hyperscaler + utility gas-turbine + grid orders.
company_layer.note: Gas turbines are sold out for years — the 'new-gas' answer to AI load.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 2026 guide $3.4–3.8B (+80%); reached GAAP profit in Q1'26. Behind-the-meter SOFC power, deployable in ~90 days.
company_layer.key_contracts: Oracle 2.8 GW Project Jupiter; Brookfield $5B framework; AEP $2.65B.
company_layer.note: Behind-the-meter power bypasses the 7–10-yr grid queue; +400% LTM.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: UK SOFC IP licensor (royalty model); £45M FY26 contracted. Doosan + Weichai are mass-production licensees. Reports GBP.
company_layer.key_contracts: Doosan Fuel Cell + Weichai licenses (mass production).
company_layer.note: Asset-light licensor; +237% YTD on AI-power optionality.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Ceres licensee; mass production from Jul'25; first 9 MW commercial Dec'25. Pure-play fuel cell. Reports KRW.
company_layer.key_contracts: Korean SOFC deployments (Ceres tech).
company_layer.note: Korea fuel-cell pure-play; tiny, early-stage.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Q1'26 revenue $30.5M (+61%); 12.5 MW DC power block (Mar'26). Pure-play, sub-scale, cash-burning.
company_layer.key_contracts: 1.5 GW DC pipeline (early).
company_layer.note: Highest-risk fuel-cell name; speculative.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Naval nuclear base + BANR SMR purpose-built for data centers; backlog $7.3B (+50%).
company_layer.key_contracts: US Navy base; BANR SMR for DC power.
company_layer.note: Only profitable, revenue-generating SMR-exposed name.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Pure power utility — AI is a demand driver, not a reported segment. 33 GW nuclear (#1 US); ~30% op margin (utility-style).
company_layer.key_contracts: Microsoft Three Mile Island restart; Meta Clinton 1,121 MW.
company_layer.note: Clean baseload is the scarce input hyperscalers are contracting for.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 44 GW nuclear + gas; AI is demand driver, not a segment. Comanche Peak nuclear.
company_layer.key_contracts: AWS + Meta PPAs.
company_layer.note: Gas + nuclear mix gives flexibility for DC load.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 10 GW merchant (Susquehanna nuclear); AI is demand driver.
company_layer.key_contracts: AWS 960 MW Pennsylvania (Susquehanna co-location).
company_layer.note: Pioneer of behind-the-meter nuclear co-location with a hyperscaler.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: 70 GW (#1 US renewables); AI is demand driver. Most Google/Meta renewable PPAs.
company_layer.key_contracts: Google + Meta renewable PPAs.
company_layer.note: Renewables + storage fill the gap while nuclear/gas lag.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Uranium fuel for the nuclear restart/expansion AI is driving; Westinghouse stake. Reports CAD.
company_layer.key_contracts: Long-term uranium supply contracts (utilities).
company_layer.note: Upstream play on the AI-nuclear thesis.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: YORK chillers + building systems; DC a growing slice of a large diversified base. Invested in Accelsius (two-phase cooling).
company_layer.key_contracts: DC chiller plant supply.
company_layer.note: Diversified building-systems giant; DC is incremental.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Chillers + thermal management; DC a growing slice of HVAC revenue.
company_layer.key_contracts: DC chiller + thermal supply.
company_layer.note: HVAC leader; AI/DC is a tailwind, not the core.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: DC cooling a small but fast-growing slice of Daikin's ~¥4.5T (~$30B) HVAC revenue; FY30 North America DC target ~$2B. DDC + Chilldyne liquid cooling (2025). Reports JPY.
company_layer.key_contracts: DDC + Chilldyne direct-liquid-cooling for DC.
company_layer.note: Global HVAC #1; liquid cooling is the AI-levered new segment.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: EMCOR is a ~$16B-revenue electrical/mechanical contractor; its fast-growing Network & Communications + mechanical-services work is increasingly data-center-driven (a slice of a diversified book).
company_layer.key_contracts: Multi-year DC electrical/mechanical build contracts (not individually disclosed).
company_layer.note: Lower-multiple, diversified contractor exposure to the buildout; not a pure AI name.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Meta sells no external cloud — its enormous capex builds captive infrastructure for Llama + ad/recommendation AI. Shown here as a top-5 capex spender (~$135–145B 2026E), not a cloud vendor. Revenue is ~98% advertising.
company_layer.key_contracts: Internal (Llama training, ad-ranking AI); CoreWeave ~$14B, Nebius ~$3B for overflow capacity.
company_layer.note: Included for capex comparability; its 'cloud revenue share' is ~nil because it is not a cloud seller.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Tencent Cloud is ~12% of Tencent's revenue (est.) and the #2 Chinese cloud behind Alibaba; bundled with the Hunyuan model and WeChat ecosystem. Group revenue is dominated by games + fintech + ads.
company_layer.key_contracts: Hunyuan model + domestic enterprise cloud.
company_layer.note: China cloud peer to Alibaba; AI cloud growing but small vs the group.

company_layer.segment_rev_note: Owns Qwen (100%) — the leading open-weight family — bundled with Alibaba Cloud. China's strongest open-model + cloud combination.
company_layer.key_contracts: Qwen open-weight family bundled with Alibaba Cloud.
company_layer.note: China's strongest open-model + cloud combination; ~2.9% of global LLM revenue (Counterpoint).
